description:
A researcher is preparing a quick performance-benchmark report that summarises how many text lines are stored in each of three raw-data directories. Everything must be done from the command line inside the /home/user home directory. Directories & starting data 1. /home/user/data/raw/dataset_A • file1.csv • file2.csv 2. /home/user/data/raw/dataset_B • data.csv 3. /home/user/data/raw/dataset_C • part1.csv • part2.csv • part3.csv Goal 1. Create the directory /home/user/data/benchmark (if it does not already exist). 2. Inside that directory create the file linecount_benchmark.csv. 3. The file must contain exactly three lines – one for each dataset – sorted alphabetically by the directory name. • Each line must have the dataset directory’s basename, followed by a comma, followed by the **total number of lines** found in **all “.csv” files directly inside that dataset directory** (non-recursive). • Do not add a header row or any extra whitespace. • The required final file format is therefore: 
dataset_A,13
dataset_B,10
dataset_C,20
Constraints & hints • Use standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities only. • The agent will have typical user-level permissions (no sudo). • Any intermediate output printed to the terminal is fine, but only the above exact content must appear in linecount_benchmark.csv. • Make sure the benchmark directory exists before you attempt to write the file; if it is missing, create it. • Count every line in the “.csv” files, including header lines, and ignore any non-CSV files (there are none by default). The automated checker will verify: • Existence of /home/user/data/benchmark/linecount_benchmark.csv. • Exact byte-for-byte match with the three-line format shown above (including newline characters after each line). • Presence of no additional files in /home/user/data/benchmark besides the one created.
truth:
Initial on-disk structure and contents available to the agent:

    /home/user/data/raw/dataset_A/file1.csv  (5 lines total)
        id,value
        1,a
        2,b
        3,c
        4,d

    /home/user/data/raw/dataset_A/file2.csv  (8 lines total)
        id,value
        1,e
        2,f
        3,g
        4,h
        5,i
        6,j
        7,k

    /home/user/data/raw/dataset_B/data.csv   (10 lines total)
        id,amount
        1,10
        2,20
        3,30
        4,40
        5,50
        6,60
        7,70
        8,80
        9,90

    /home/user/data/raw/dataset_C/part1.csv  (4 lines total)
        id,sales
        1,100
        2,150
        3,200

    /home/user/data/raw/dataset_C/part2.csv  (6 lines total)
        id,sales
        1,300
        2,350
        3,400
        4,450
        5,500

    /home/user/data/raw/dataset_C/part3.csv  (10 lines total)
        id,sales
        1,550
        2,600
        3,650
        4,700
        5,750
        6,800
        7,850
        8,900
        9,950

    Expected final artefact created by the agent:

    /home/user/data/benchmark/linecount_benchmark.csv  
    └─ Content (including trailing newline characters):
        dataset_A,13\n
        dataset_B,10\n
        dataset_C,20\n
